root_text.sn39.16:1.1: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, imasmiṁ dhammavinaye dukkaran”ti?

translation_text.sn39.16:1.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, in this teaching and training, what is hard to do?”
translation_text.sn39.16:1.2: “Going forth, reverend, is hard to do in this teaching and training.”
translation_text.sn39.16:1.3: “But what’s hard to do for someone who has gone forth?”
translation_text.sn39.16:1.4: “When you’ve gone forth it’s hard to be satisfied.”
translation_text.sn39.16:1.5: “But what’s hard to do for someone who is satisfied?”
translation_text.sn39.16:1.6: “When you’re satisfied, it’s hard to practice in line with the teaching.”
translation_text.sn39.16:1.7: “But if a mendicant practices  in line with the teaching, will it take them long to become a perfected one?”
translation_text.sn39.16:1.11: The Linked Discourses with Sāmaṇḍaka are complete.
